content.text:
*"From Him the whole body, joined and held together by 
every supporting ligament, grows and builds itself up in love."*{Ephesians 4:16}{Graph theory began with Euler and his bridges; it now touches every corner of mathematics and computer science.}{Leonhard Euler}

A **graph** is a mathematical structure representing 
connections between objects.

content.title: Graph
content.content:
A **graph** $G = (V, E)$ consists of:

- $V$: a nonempty set of **vertices** (or nodes)

- $E$: a set of **edges**, each connecting two vertices

An edge between $u$ and $v$ is denoted $\{u, v\}$ or simply $uv$.

content.title: Directed Graph
content.content:
A **directed graph** (digraph) has edges with 
direction: an edge from $u$ to $v$ is denoted $(u, v)$ and is different 
from $(v, u)$.

content.title: Social network
content.problem:
Let vertices represent people and edges represent friendships:

$V = \{\text{Alice}, \text{Bob}, \text{Carol}, \text{Dave}\}$

$E = \{\{\text{Alice}, \text{Bob}\}, \{\text{Bob}, \text{Carol}\}, \{\text{Alice}, \text{Carol}\}\}$

Dave has no friends in this network.

content.title: Basic Terms
content.content:
- **Adjacent**: Vertices $u$ and $v$ are adjacent if $\{u, v\} \in E$.

- **Incident**: Edge $e$ is incident to vertex $v$ if $v$ is an endpoint of $e$.

- **Degree**: $\deg(v)$ = number of edges incident to $v$.

- **Loop**: An edge from a vertex to itself.

- **Multiple edges**: More than one edge between the same pair.

- **Simple graph**: No loops or multiple edges.
Each edge contributes 2 to the total degree (one for each endpoint).

content.title: Handshaking corollary
content.problem:
In any graph, the number of vertices with odd degree is even.

proof
Let $O$ = vertices of odd degree, $E$ = vertices of even degree.

$\sum_{v \in O} \deg(v) + \sum_{v \in E} \deg(v) = 2|E|$ (even).

The sum over $E$ is even, so the sum over $O$ must be even.

Sum of odd numbers is even only if there are evenly many of them.
proof

items: **Complete graph** $K_n$: every pair of $n$ vertices is connected.
**Bipartite graph**: vertices split into two groups; edges only between groups.
**Complete bipartite** $K_{m,n}$: all possible edges between groups of $m$ and $n$.

marginNotes.text:
A cord of three strands is not 
quickly broken.
